# The Elixir Language — F3

> The chapter that grounds the functional ideas of F2 in real Elixir: values and IEx, pattern matching, modules, enumerables and streams, structs, protocols, processes, and OTP. Start with the language's history, a release timeline, and a look under the hood.

Chapter map for LLMs/agents. Hub: [/elixir/language](/elixir/language). Links are clean root-relative URLs; this file lists every lesson in the chapter in course order.

## Lessons

- [Values, types & IEx — F3.01](/elixir/language/values): The data Elixir is built from — integers, floats, atoms, booleans, strings, lists, tuples, and maps — explored through IEx, the interactive shell, and the i/1 helper.
- [A short history of Elixir — F3](/elixir/language/history): Why José Valim built Elixir on the Erlang VM in 2011, what it inherited from Erlang, Ruby, and Clojure, and how it reached a stable 1.0 in 2014.
- [The Elixir release timeline — F3](/elixir/language/timeline): An interactive timeline of Elixir's milestones, from the first commit in 2011 to the current stable release, with one headline feature per version.
- [Under the hood — F3](/elixir/language/under-the-hood): How Elixir source becomes BEAM bytecode: tokenizing, parsing to the quoted AST, macro expansion, and the Erlang VM that runs the result.
- [Pattern matching & the match operator — F3.02](/elixir/language/match): Pattern matching is how Elixir reads the shape of data and pulls it apart. This module introduces it through the project the whole course builds: a learning portal with…
  - [The match operator — F3.02](/elixir/language/match/operator): = asserts that a value matches a pattern and binds the rest; the pin operator ^ matches against a value you already have. Seen through verifying a magic-link sign-in.
  - [Destructuring portal data — F3.02](/elixir/language/match/destructuring): Pulling fields out of tuples, lists, maps, and structs in a single match — the auth claims, request params, and progress records the learning portal passes around.
  - [Branching with case, with & guards — F3.02](/elixir/language/match/branching): Dispatching on shape: function-head matching, case, guards, and the with pipeline — built around the portal's magic-link sign-in flow and its progress events.
- [Enumerables & streams — F3.04](/elixir/language/enum-streams): A collection is anything that implements the Enumerable protocol; Enum walks it eagerly, and Stream walks it lazily. This module deepens the Enum steps from the pipe and…
  - [Enum, the eager workhorse — F3.04](/elixir/language/enum-streams/enum): The Enumerable protocol unifies lists, ranges, maps, and streams, and the Enum module is the toolkit that walks them — map, filter, reduce, group_by, frequencies — each…
  - [Comprehensions — F3.04](/elixir/language/enum-streams/comprehensions): The for comprehension: generators draw from any enumerable, filters drop items, :into chooses the result collection, and multiple generators nest — set-builder notation…
  - [Lazy streams — F3.04](/elixir/language/enum-streams/streams): Stream builds a lazy recipe that computes nothing until an Enum function pulls values through — the same pipeline eager and lazy, early exit, infinite sequences, and…
- [Functions, modules & the pipe — F3.03](/elixir/language/modules): Functions are the unit of work, modules group them, and the pipe composes them. This module builds the learning portal's first real modules — Accounts, Auth, Catalog…
  - [Defining functions — F3.03](/elixir/language/modules/functions): Named functions with def and defp, multiple clauses that dispatch by pattern and guard, arity, anonymous functions, and the capture operator — seen through the portal's…
  - [Organising with modules — F3.03](/elixir/language/modules/organising): defmodule, module attributes, alias and import, and documentation — how the Portal namespace is structured and how its modules refer to one another.
  - [The pipe operator — F3.03](/elixir/language/modules/pipe): |> threads a value as the first argument to the next call, turning nested calls into a readable pipeline — composing Portal and Enum functions over a learner's progress.
- [OTP: GenServer & supervisors — F3.08](/elixir/language/otp): OTP wraps the actor model in tested patterns: a GenServer holds state behind callbacks, a Supervisor restarts crashed children — an OTP system as a small tree of server…
  - [The GenServer behaviour — F3.08.1](/elixir/language/otp/genserver): A GenServer abstracts the receive loop into a behaviour: init/1 sets the state, handle_call/3 answers synchronous requests, handle_cast/2 handles asynchronous ones, and…
  - [Synchronous call, asynchronous cast — F3.08.2](/elixir/language/otp/call-cast): GenServer.call sends a request and blocks for the reply, routing to handle_call; GenServer.cast returns :ok at once, routing to handle_cast; a clean client API wraps…
  - [Supervisors & restart strategies — F3.08.3](/elixir/language/otp/supervisors): A supervisor starts child processes and restarts them when they crash, by strategy — one_for_one, one_for_all, or rest_for_one — turning process isolation into recovery…
- [The process playground — F3.09](/elixir/language/playground): The F3 capstone lab: a live supervised tree you drive — send messages into a worker's mailbox, drain them through its receive loop to move state, issue a synchronous…
- [Processes & the actor model — F3.07](/elixir/language/processes): A process is the BEAM's isolated unit of concurrency, coordinating only by messages — the actor model built from three primitives: spawn a process, send and receive…
  - [Spawning a process — F3.07.1](/elixir/language/processes/spawn): spawn/1 starts a function as a new process and returns a PID at once; the child runs concurrently on its own heap, and a crash stays inside its boundary — the isolation…
  - [Sending & receiving messages — F3.07.2](/elixir/language/processes/messages): send/2 appends a term to a mailbox and returns; receive pattern-matches messages out, leaving unmatched ones queued; a message carries self() so the server can reply…
  - [Holding state in a loop — F3.07.3](/elixir/language/processes/state): A process holds state as the argument to a recursive receive loop, tail-calling itself with the updated value after each message — a hand-written GenServer, and the…
- [Protocols & behaviours — F3.06](/elixir/language/protocols): Two kinds of polymorphism in Elixir: a protocol dispatches a function on a value's type at runtime, a behaviour is a compile-time contract a module fulfils — with the…
  - [Defining a protocol — F3.06.1](/elixir/language/protocols/define): defprotocol declares a contract of function signatures; a call resolves to the implementation registered for the value's type, dispatching by tag, or raises…
  - [Implementing for a struct — F3.06.2](/elixir/language/protocols/defimpl): defimpl Protocol, for: Struct gives the per-type bodies a call resolves to; three implementations form a dispatch table that grows by addition — open for extension…
  - [Behaviours & callbacks — F3.06.3](/elixir/language/protocols/behaviours): @callback declares a typed contract on a module; @behaviour and @impl true fulfil it and let the compiler flag a missing callback — the compile-time counterpart to a…
- [Structs, maps & keyword lists — F3.05](/elixir/language/structs): Three containers for key-and-value data — the map, the keyword list, and the struct — and when each fits, with the portal's User as the running example.
  - [Defining a struct — F3.05.1](/elixir/language/structs/define): defstruct over Portal.Accounts.User: the %User{} literal as sugar over a map, and the hidden __struct__ key that names the module and makes a struct an ordinary map at…
  - [Enforcing keys & defaults — F3.05.2](/elixir/language/structs/defaults): @enforce_keys for required fields and keyword defaults in defstruct: what fills in for the common case, and the ArgumentError raised at construction when an essential…
  - [Matching on a struct's type — F3.05.3](/elixir/language/structs/matching): The %Struct{} pattern dispatches on the __struct__ tag across function clauses; why clause order matters when a struct is a map, and the is_struct/2 guard that keeps…
